title: महाकाली कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री महाकाली कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायेत्कालीं महाकालीं कालिकां कालरूपिणीम्।
चतुर्भुजां मुण्डमालां खड्गहस्तां भयानकाम्॥

रक्तजिह्वां ललज्जिह्वां श्मशानस्थां दिगम्बराम्।
महाकालप्रियां देवीं महाकालीं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु काली च ललाटं कालरात्रिका।
चक्षुषी भद्रकाली च कर्णौ रक्षतु चामुण्डा॥ १॥

नासिकां पातु शाकम्भरी ओष्ठौ रक्षतु चण्डिका।
जिह्वां रक्षतु चण्डी च दन्तान् रक्षतु मातृका॥ २॥

कण्ठं रक्षतु कात्यायनी स्कन्धौ पातु शिवप्रिया।
बाहू रक्षतु भैरवी करौ रक्षतु दक्षिणा॥ ३॥

हृदयं पातु कौशिकी नाभिं रक्षतु शाम्भवी।
कटिं रक्षतु दुर्गा च ऊरू रक्षतु काळिका॥ ४॥

जानुनी पातु शूलिनी जङ्घे रक्षतु चण्डिका।
पादौ पातु महामाया सर्वाङ्गं पातु सर्वदा॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु वाराही आग्नेय्यां रक्षतु रौद्रिणी।
दक्षिणे रक्षतु प्रेता नैऋत्यां पातु भैरवी॥ ६॥

पश्चिमे रक्षतु शूली वायव्यां सिंहवाहिनी।
उत्तरे रक्षतु ज्वाला ऐशान्यां शूलधारिणी॥ ७॥

ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु माहेश्वरी अधो रक्षतु पातालवासिनी।
सर्वतः सर्वकालं च महाकाली सदावतु॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
महाकालीकवचं यः पठेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
न तस्य जायते भीतिर्न रोगो न च विपत्तयः॥

सर्वशत्रुविनाशं च सर्वरक्षाकरं परम्।
महाकालीप्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री महाकाली कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mahakali Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Dhyayet-Kalim Mahakalim Kalikam Kala-rupinim.
Chaturbhujam Munda-malam Khadga-hastam Bhayanakam.

Rakta-jihvam Lalaj-jihvam Shmashana-stham Digambaram.
Mahakala-priyam Devim Mahakalim Namamyaham.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Kali Cha Lalatam Kalaratrika.
Chakshushi Bhadrakali Cha Karnau Rakshatu Chamunda. || 1 ||

Nasikam Patu Shakambhari Oshthau Rakshatu Chandika.
Jihvam Rakshatu Chandi Cha Dantan Rakshatu Matrika. || 2 ||

Kantham Rakshatu Katyayani Skandhau Patu Shivapriya.
Bahu Rakshatu Bhairavi Karau Rakshatu Dakshina. || 3 ||

Hridayam Patu Kaushiki Nabhim Rakshatu Shambhavi.
Katim Rakshatu Durga Cha Uru Rakshatu Kalika. || 4 ||

Januni Patu Shulini Janghe Rakshatu Chandika.
Padau Patu Mahamaya Sarvangam Patu Sarvada. || 5 ||

Prachyam Rakshatu Varahi Agneyyam Rakshatu Raudrini.
Dakshine Rakshatu Preta Nairityam Patu Bhairavi. || 6 ||

Pashchime Rakshatu Shuli Vayavyam Simhavahini.
Uttare Rakshatu Jwala Aishanyam Shula-dharini. || 7 ||

Urdhvam Rakshatu Maheshwari Adho Rakshatu Patala-vasini.
Sarvatah Sarvakalam Cha Mahakali Sadavatu. || 8 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Mahakali-kavacham Yah Pathed-bhakti-samanvitah.
Na Tasya Jayate Bhitir-na Rogo Na Cha Vipattayah.

Sarva-shatru-vinasham Cha Sarva-raksha-karam Param.
Mahakali-prasadena Sarva-siddhim-avaapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Mahakali Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: महाकाली कवचम् में देवी काली के विभिन्न रूपों द्वारा शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग और सभी दिशाओं की रक्षा का वर्णन है। शिर की रक्षा काली, ललाट की कालरात्रि, नेत्रों की भद्रकाली, कानों की चामुण्डा, हृदय की कौशिकी, कटि की दुर्गा, पादों की महामाया करती हैं। आठों दिशाओं में वाराही, रौद्रिणी, भैरवी, शूलिनी, सिंहवाहिनी, ज्वाला आदि देवियाँ रक्षा करती हैं। जो भक्तिपूर्वक इसका पाठ करता है उसे भय, रोग और विपत्ति नहीं होती।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish an impenetrable divine armor of goddess mahakali
Protects every body part through different forms of Devi
Guards all eight directions from negative energies
Traditionally recited for removal of fear, disease, and all calamities
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and grants complete siddhi
description: Mahakali Kavacham — महाकाली कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Goddess Kali. This sacred composition is sourced from Kalika Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to remove fear, disease, and all calamities, destroy all enemies and grant complete siddhi, and protect every body part through different forms of Devi. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kali Puja and Navratri for enhanced blessings. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Mahakali Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mahakali Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armor prayer to Goddess Mahakali for full-body protection from fears and enemies.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mahakali Kavacham and which form of the Goddess does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahakali Kavacham is a Sanskrit kavacha (protective armour) prayer dedicated to Mahakali — the cosmic-scale supreme form of Kali invoked in the Devi Mahatmya (Saptashati) Chapter 1, where she emerges from Vishnu's yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep) at Brahma's stuti to slay the demons Madhu and Kaitabha at the very beginning of creation. The kavacha is preserved in the Kalika Purana and encloses each limb of the chanter in Mahakali's protective grace. While Kali is the daily-worshipped form, Mahakali is her supreme cosmic aspect, the first of the Devi-trinity (Mahakali-Mahalakshmi-Mahasaraswati) governing tamas, sattva and rajas respectively. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya 1; Kalika Purana; Hindupedia Mahakali.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Mahakali differ from Kali in iconography and theology?
seoFaqs.answer: Kali (the first Mahavidya, from the Bhairava-Tantra Dakshina Kali tradition) is depicted with four arms, dark-blue or black, standing on Shiva's body — the goddess of personal sadhana and intimate devotion. Mahakali (the cosmic form from Devi Mahatmya 1) is depicted with ten arms (some texts give twelve or eighteen), each holding a different weapon (sword, discus, mace, conch, trident, bow, spear, club, etc.), three eyes, dark-complexioned but radiating cosmic-scale energy, surrounded by vast hosts of yoginis, capable of overwhelming entire universes. Theologically Mahakali is the tamas-aspect of the Tridevi (alongside Mahalakshmi as sattva and Mahasaraswati as rajas), the destructive cosmic force, while Kali is her accessible Mahavidya manifestation. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya 1; Hindupedia Tridevi; Kalika Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Madhu-Kaitabha episode that frames Mahakali's appearance?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Mahatmya opens with King Suratha and the merchant Samadhi approaching Sage Medhas, who recounts how at the very beginning of creation, when Vishnu was reposing on the cosmic ocean in yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep) on Sheshanaga, two demons Madhu and Kaitabha arose from his ear-wax and threatened Brahma seated on the lotus from Vishnu's navel. Brahma stuti'd Mahakali — the personified yoga-nidra herself — who withdrew from Vishnu, awakening him. Vishnu then engaged the demons in five thousand years of combat, and finally bewildered them with Mahakali's mohini-shakti so that they offered him a boon, which Vishnu used to slay them on his own thigh (the only place not covered by water, a condition the demons themselves had set). (Sources: Devi Mahatmya 1; Markandeya Purana; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Does Mahakali Kavacham require Tantric initiation (diksha) to chant?
seoFaqs.answer: Unlike the more esoteric Mahavidya kavachas, the Mahakali Kavacham is generally accessible to mainstream Hindu devotees because it derives from the Devi Mahatmya — the universal Shakta scripture recited daily during Navaratri across all sampradayas without diksha requirements. The Devi Mahatmya tradition explicitly democratises Devi worship. However, the longer Tantric mool mantra associated with Mahakali (varying by lineage) and any purascharana sadhana with extensive japa of Kali bijas should still be undertaken with diksha from a qualified guru. The kavacha alone, recited during Navaratri or daily as part of Devi Mahatmya saptashati paath, is considered safe and beneficial for all sincere devotees. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya tradition; Kalika Purana; Hindupedia Devi sadhana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Mahakali Kavacham be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal occasion is Navaratri, especially the Krishna-paksha Sharad Navaratri's seventh night (Saptami) traditionally associated with Kalaratri-Mahakali, and the eighth night (Mahashtami) when Mahakali is invoked in her supreme form. The kavacha is also chanted during the entire Ashvina month, every Tuesday and Saturday, on Krishna Chaturdashi (especially Margashirsha Krishna Chaturdashi), at lunar and solar eclipses, on Kali Puja (Kartik Amavasya), and during the Phalguna Kali Puja. Brahma-muhurta and the deep night are the most efficacious hours. Devotees facing existential threats — serious illness, court cases involving life and liberty, persistent enemies, family destruction — recite forty-day mandalas. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navaratri calendar; Hindupedia Mahakali days; Devi Mahatmya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Mahakali Kavacham phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the kavacha grants protection from all cosmic-scale dangers — wars, epidemics, natural disasters, political upheavals, large-scale hostilities — that exceed what individual deities normally address. It grants victory over insurmountable enemies, fearlessness in the face of death, immunity from black magic and tantric attacks, removal of multi-generational curses, the destruction of inner demons (kama, krodha, lobha, moha, mada, matsarya), and ultimately the supreme realisation of one's identity with the cosmic Shakti. Regular recitation during Navaratri is held to grant exceptional spiritual transformation. The kavacha is considered the most powerful Devi armour for crisis periods. (Sources: Kalika Purana phalashruti; Devi Mahatmya tradition; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the major Mahakali temples in India?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prominent Mahakali shrines include: the Mahakali Mata temple at Pavagadh near Vadodara, Gujarat (one of the 51 Shakta pithas, where Sati's toes fell, atop the Pavagadh hill); the Kalika Mata temple at Patan, Gujarat; the Mahakali shrine at Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh (associated with the Mahakal-Mahakali pair); the Mahakali Mata temple at Chandrapur, Maharashtra; the Adi Shakti Mahakali temple at Mount Abu; the Mahakali shrine within the Kamakhya complex (Mahavidya pithas); and the Mahakali Devi temple at Bahla, Karnataka. The Devi Mahatmya saptashati paath performed at any of these temples during Navaratri is considered exceptionally fruitful. (Sources: Wikipedia Mahakali temples; Hindupedia Shakta pithas; Pavagadh temple guide.)
